messages.meaning: portmgrd successfully applied an MTU value to a kernel network interface via ip link set mtu
likely_causes: Operator applied 'interface mtu <value>' in CONFIG_DB
Automated provisioning pushed a new MTU via RESTAPI or sonic-cfggen
next_steps: Confirm the MTU is active on the kernel interface: ip link show <interface> | grep mtu
Verify CONFIG_DB entry: redis-cli -n 4 HGET 'PORT|<interface>' mtu
Check APPL_DB was updated: redis-cli -n 0 HGET 'PORT_TABLE:<interface>' mtu
If traffic issues persist after MTU change, check end-to-end path MTU: ping -s <size> -M do <peer>

messages.meaning: portmgrd applied an admin-up or admin-down to the kernel interface in response to a CONFIG_DB change
likely_causes: Operator ran 'interface shutdown' or 'no shutdown' via CLI or SONiC config
Automated rollout toggling ports during provisioning
Warm restart replay of admin state from CONFIG_DB
next_steps: Verify the kernel state matches intent: ip link show <interface>
Check CONFIG_DB admin_status: redis-cli -n 4 HGET 'PORT|<interface>' admin_status
Confirm orchagent propagated the change to ASIC: redis-cli -n 0 HGET 'PORT_TABLE:<interface>' admin_status
If port should be up but kernel shows DOWN, check physical link: show interfaces status <interface>
Review recent config changes: show logging | grep portmgrd | tail -20

messages.pattern: Failed to set.*MTU|Failed to set port MTU|error.*mtu.*EOPNOTSUPP|EINVAL.*mtu
messages.meaning: portmgrd attempted to apply an MTU value to a kernel interface but the kernel or driver rejected it
likely_causes: Requested MTU exceeds the driver maximum for this NIC/ASIC (common above 9216 bytes)
Interface is in a bonded/LAG group whose master has a lower MTU
Kernel module does not support jumbo frames
Race condition: interface does not exist in kernel yet when portmgrd tried to configure it
next_steps: Check kernel error details: journalctl -u portmgrd | grep -i mtu | tail -30
Verify driver MTU limits: ip link show <interface> | grep mtu; ethtool -i <interface>
Check if interface is in a portchannel: show interfaces portchannel
If LAG member, set MTU on the portchannel itself, not the member
Confirm platform MTU support in platform.json or hwsku.json
Fallback: set MTU manually to confirm kernel accepts it: ip link set <intf> mtu <value>; if rejected, the value exceeds hardware capability

messages.meaning: portmgrd wrote a new port speed (or autoneg setting) to the kernel interface or notified orchagent via APPL_DB
likely_causes: Operator set interface speed via CLI or config_db.json
Autoneg mode changed alongside speed hint
next_steps: Verify speed is visible: show interfaces status <interface>
Check APPL_DB: redis-cli -n 0 HGET 'PORT_TABLE:<interface>' speed
Check ASIC_DB to confirm SAI programmed it: redis-cli -n 1 KEYS 'ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_PORT:*' | xargs -I{} redis-cli -n 1 HGET {} SAI_PORT_ATTR_SPEED
If speed mismatch persists, verify autoneg setting: show interfaces autoneg status <interface>
Check transceiver supports requested speed: show interfaces transceiver eeprom <interface>

messages.meaning: portmgrd applied a Forward Error Correction mode change to the port, writing it to APPL_DB for orchagent/syncd to program into the ASIC
likely_causes: Operator set 'interface fec <mode>' (none, rs, fc) via CLI
Autoneg negotiated a new FEC mode with the peer
next_steps: Verify current FEC mode: show interfaces fec status <interface>
Check APPL_DB: redis-cli -n 0 HGET 'PORT_TABLE:<interface>' fec
Confirm orchagent programmed the SAI attribute: docker exec swss grep -i fec /var/log/swss/orchagent.log | tail -20
If BER increases after FEC change, check cable quality: show interfaces transceiver eeprom <interface>
RS-FEC required for 100G+ ports on most platforms -- do not disable without careful testing

messages.meaning: portmgrd issued a netlink request to configure the port but received an error response from the kernel
likely_causes: Interface does not exist in the kernel at the time of the netlink call (timing/ordering issue)
Kernel returned ENODEV -- interface was deleted or renamed
Insufficient privileges (should not happen inside swss container but can after custom hardening)
Conflicting configuration from another daemon or script running outside SONiC orchestration
next_steps: Check if interface exists in kernel: ip link show <interface>
Review portmgrd logs for the preceding context: docker exec swss grep portmgrd /var/log/syslog | tail -50
Restart portmgrd if interface is present but state is stale: docker exec swss supervisorctl restart portmgrd
If interface is missing entirely, check if swss orchagent created it: redis-cli -n 0 HGETALL 'PORT_TABLE:<interface>'
Verify no external scripts are managing the same interface: ps aux | grep 'ip link'
As last resort, restart swss container: sudo systemctl restart swss (causes brief traffic disruption)

messages.pattern: waiting for port ready|port.*not ready|port state check.*wait
messages.meaning: portmgrd is polling STATE_DB waiting for orchagent to mark the port as ready before applying further configuration -- a normal but time-bounded wait state
likely_causes: orchagent has not yet created the SAI port object and written 'ok' to STATE_DB:PORT_TABLE
syncd is slow to respond during bootup or warm restart, delaying orchagent
portmgrd started before orchagent finished initializing ports (race at container start)
Port was removed and re-added (e.g., breakout reconfiguration) and portmgrd picked up the CONFIG_DB entry before orchagent reprovisioned it
next_steps: Check if the port is ready in STATE_DB: redis-cli -n 6 HGET 'PORT_TABLE|<interface>' state
Check orchagent is running: docker exec swss supervisorctl status orchagent
Check if orchagent is stuck: docker exec swss grep -i 'error\|fatal' /var/log/swss/orchagent.log | tail -20
Monitor STATE_DB for port readiness: watch -n 1 'redis-cli -n 6 KEYS PORT_TABLE*'
If wait exceeds 60 seconds, check syncd: docker exec syncd supervisorctl status syncd
If after breakout reconfiguration, allow extra time -- portmgrd retries on a timer and will proceed once orchagent completes

messages.meaning: portmgrd applied an interface description or alias to the kernel interface (written to /sys/class/net/<intf>/ifalias or via netlink)
likely_causes: Operator set 'interface description <text>' in CONFIG_DB
Bulk provisioning script updated interface descriptions via sonic-cfggen or config reload
next_steps: Verify alias was applied to kernel: cat /sys/class/net/<interface>/ifalias
Check CONFIG_DB description field: redis-cli -n 4 HGET 'PORT|<interface>' description
Confirm APPL_DB reflects description: redis-cli -n 0 HGET 'PORT_TABLE:<interface>' description
If description is blank but CONFIG_DB has a value, restart portmgrd: docker exec swss supervisorctl restart portmgrd
